IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: To create employment for people with disabilities.
IRS990.MissionDesc: To create employment for people with disabilities.
IRS990.Desc: See Schedule OPRIDE Industries One, Inc. creates employment for people with disabilities. As a nonprofit social enterprise, our company provides facilities operations and maintenance services, custodial services, contract manufacturing, supply chain management, and packaging and fulfillment services to private and public organizations nationwide. We are the nation's leading employer of people with disabilities and we share our expertise with other organizations aiming to build inclusive workforces. Our tailored employment services include vocational training, on-the-job support, scholarships, and paid internships. In addition to supporting people with disabilities, we also assist veterans, former foster youth, and trafficking survivors.Our programs for people with disabilities are designed to enhance personal and professional growth as well as increased independence. Each participant's plan is customized to their strengths, abilities, and goals. For the year ending June 30, 2024, PRIDE Industries One, Inc., in connection with PRIDE Industries, provided employment and services to more than 4,600 individuals with diverse intellectual, developmental, physical, sensory, or mental health disabilities. As a nonprofit social enterprise, we create jobs within our own business operation and through partnerships with other employers. Nearly 300 individuals with disabilities were employed on commercial contracts with PRIDE Industries One, Inc. In 2024, the Manufacturing and Logistics Service operations earned AS9100D certification, a quality management system standard, that enables us to serve military and aerospace industry partners.Additionally, our fleet of buses provides essential transportation for people who are unable to drive to work, training, and other vital services. In the year ending June 30, 2024, we provided over 104,000 individual rides to help people with disabilities access work, training and critical services.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: In the event the Company had a tax position that was uncertain of being sustained, the Company would apply the minimum probability threshold specified in accounting guidance on income tax accounting to determine whether a financial statement liability or benefit should be recognized. There were no uncertain tax positions at June 30, 2024 and 2023.

In 2023, as part of the relocation offer package, a total of $11,165 was approved and disbursed to the SVP of Manufacturing, Logistics and Engineering and Project Services to cover temporary housing and travel expenses for him and his companion(s). Tax gross-up payments were also incurred for the SVP of Manufacturing, Logistics and Engineering and Project Services. These benefits were included in taxable income. The company has also incurred business related expenses on a periodic basis for First Class travel for the SVP of Manufacturing, Logistics, and Engineering and Project Services. This was not included in taxable income.

The President/CEO of the organization is an employee of PRIDE Industries. The Board's Compensation Committee retains the ultimate authority over all elements of compensation to ensure that the organizational purposes are appropriately serviced. The Board's Compensation Committee uses credible data sources, third-party compensation consultant review and an objective "arms-length" decision-making process, ensuring the integrity of the executive programs and consistency with the organization's overall mission.

Casey Blake received a one-time compensation payment (the contents of which are subject to a confidentiality clause) of $170,400, which is included on Schedule J, Part II, Column B(iii). Key executives participate in a 457(f) retirement plan. Plan eligibility is determined upon approval by the Board of Directors of PRIDE Industries. During the calendar year ended December 31, 2023, contributions and distributions for the following key executives are as follows: Jeffery Dern - President/CEO: Contributions: $36,500 Distributions: None Casey Blake - Chief Operations Officer (Termed 02/2023): Contributions: None Distributions: $37,884 Charles Sharp - Chief Financial Officer: Contributions: $20,000 Distributions: None The President/CEO participates in a nonqualified deferred compensation trust as provided for in his employment agreement approved by the Board of Directors. Contributions to the trust are made annually, each December, in an amount equal to 7.5% of the President/CEO's base salary. The trust is distributable upon the earlier of the President/CEO reaching age 65 or has a qualifying termination event. Certain forfeiture provisions exist in the agreement. During the Calendar year ended December 31, 2023, a contribution of $39,750 was made. In 2021, the President/CEO commenced participation in a long-term incentive compensation program. The Board's Compensation Committee annually approves incentive amounts ranging from 0% to 7.5% of the President/CEO's base salary, based on both individual and Company performance. These approved amounts are accrued each year and paid out in a lump sum every five years, contingent upon continued employment. For the Calendar year ended December 31, 2023, the Compensation Committee approved an accrual of $79,500, including a retroactive accrual for the year ended December 31, 2022.
The compensation reflected in this section represents the Form W-2 calendar year taxable income for these individuals. The compensation is paid by PRIDE Industries, as the employer of record.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, line 8b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PRIDE Industries One, Inc. does not have any committees of the board.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The accounting department consults with various departments in the compiling of the Form 990. In addition, a nationally recognized CPA firm consults on the preparation and review of the form. The CFO reviews and approves the data and completed form before providing it to the President/CEO and Board members for their review prior to filing.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Board members and officers are required to complete an annual disclosure form identifying business interests, employment relationships and property interests, and those of related parties. The President/CEO and Board Chair review the disclosures for potential conflicts of interest and consult legal counsel as necessary. The Board Chair may ask involved individuals to recuse themselves during discussions on any matter related to a potential conflict. No director shall vote on any issue involving a financial or personal conflict of interest, or participate in decisions in matters directly involving related parties. All key employees, including officers, are required to participate in annual training on the organization's Code of Business Ethics which includes a discussion of conflicts of interest. Training compliance is monitored by electronic acceptance and acknowledgement of the policies within the Company's electronic training platform.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: All of PRIDE Industries One, Inc.'s employees are compensated by PRIDE Industries, a related organization, as the common paymaster. Salary surveys of benchmark jobs are used to determine wage ranges of key employees and executives. Market-available wage data, job performance, and current market conditions are used to determine appropriate compensation levels. The company contracts with a leading HR consulting firm to analyze the compensation of certain executives. The Board Compensation Committee of PRIDE Industries reviews surveys from similarly sized nonprofit and for-profit organizations, taking into account relative factors including revenues, employee counts and competitive market position. The Compensation Committee of the Board reviews and approves compensation for the President/CEO and CFO. The CFO is excluded from involvement in the process.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Audited financial statements, governing documents and conflict of interest policies are made available to the general public upon request.

Consulting & Professional Fees: Program service expenses 755,669. Management and general expenses 0. Fundraising expenses 0. Total expenses 755,669. Contractor Reimbursement Expense: Program service expenses 10,800. Management and general expenses 0. Fundraising expenses 0. Total expenses 10,800. Contractor Labor: Program service expenses 596,356. Management and general expenses 0. Fundraising expenses 0. Total expenses 596,356. Subcontractor Expense: Program service expenses 11,710,899. Management and general expenses 0. Fundraising expenses 0. Total expenses 11,710,899. Contract Disposal Services: Program service expenses 164,605. Management and general expenses 0. Fundraising expenses 0. Total expenses 164,605. Other Service Fees: Program service expenses 42,133,468. Management and general expenses 0. Fundraising expenses 0. Total expenses 42,133,468.

The compensation expense for PRIDE Industries One, Inc. is paid by PRIDE Industries. The company's compensation expense on Form 990, Part IX reflects 1,985 employees who are paid by PRIDE Industries as the common paymaster.

IdRelatedTaxExemptOrgGrp.PrimaryActivitiesTxt: To create employment for people with disabilities
Helping people with disabilities prepare for employment
